explanation: The rule authors explicitly removed '\cmd.exe' from the ParentImage selection list (noted as '# - '\cmd.exe' # too many false positives'). Using cmd.exe as the parent process to spawn the targeted child processes completely avoids the selection criteria.

The rule contains a broad exclusion named 'filter_nessus' that drops the alert if the CommandLine contains the string '\nessus_'. Appending this specific string as a comment or dummy argument forces the logic to ignore the event.

The detection relies on statically matching the end of the executed Image path (e.g., '\certutil.exe'). Copying the native binary to a different name before execution breaks the 'Image|endswith' logic.

The rule includes an exclusion 'filter_ccmcache' which suppresses the alert if the CurrentDirectory contains '\ccmcache\'. Executing the suspicious child process from within this working directory will bypass the rule.

Bypass by parent process selection: The Sigma rule explicitly comments out '\cmd.exe' from the ParentImage list to reduce false positives. Spawning a targeted child process (like certutil.exe) directly from cmd.exe evades this rule.
